items.name: Equipment:
items.entry: Choose A or B: (A) {@item Book|XPHB}, {@item Calligrapher's Supplies|XPHB}, {@item Ink|XPHB}, {@item Ink|XPHB} Pen, {@item Parchment|XPHB} (10 sheets), {@item Traveler's Clothes|XPHB}, 2 GP; or (B) 50 GP.

backgroundFluff.name: Pact Seeker
entries: You had a purpose in mind. Perhaps you were seeking out knowledge or power. Or maybe you were in search of hidden secrets. One way or another, your journey lead you to seek out an extraplanar entity. Once you made contact, a deal was struck, and your life changed.

feat.name: Fey Pact
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Fey Bond
entries: You know Sylvan. If you already know Sylvan when you select this feat, you instead learn one language of your choice from the language tables in the Player's Handbook. You also gain {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB} in the {@skill Nature|XPHB} skill.

entries.name: Fey Cantrips
entries: You know the {@spell Druidcraft|XPHB} cantrip and learn one other cantrip of your choice from the Divination or Enchantment school of magic. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this feat).

entries.name: Honeyed Words
entries: When you roll a 5 or lower on the {@dice d20} for a Charisma ({@skill Deception|XPHB}) or Charisma ({@skill Persuasion|XPHB}) check, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to reroll the check, and you must use the new roll. Once you take this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, you can't use this benefit again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

feat.name: Fey Sentinel
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Fading Target
entries: When an enemy misses you with an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to gain the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn or until immediately after you make an attack roll, deal damage, or cast a spell. Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Fey Shift
entries: When you take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action, you can forgo the extra movement to teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within a distance equal to half your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} instead.

entries.name: Nature's Roots
entries: You always have the {@spell Entangle|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast it using any spell slots you have. The spell's spellcasting ability is the ability increased by this feat.
When you reach character level 5, you also always have the {@spell Plant Growth|XPHB} spell prepared and can cast it the same way.

feat.name: Infernal Bulwark
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Devil's Flesh
entries: Your skin thickens and assumes a scaled, leathery texture. While you aren't wearing armor or wielding a {@item Shield|XPHB}, your base {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} equals 10 plus your Dexterity modifier plus the modifier of the ability increased by this feat.

entries.name: Infernal Protection
entries: You always have the {@spell Armor of Agathys|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. When you cast it without a spell slot using this feature, you cast this spell as its level 2 version, and it deals Fire damage instead of Cold damage. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

entries.name: Vengeful Surge
entries: Whenever a creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself forces you to make a saving throw and you succeed, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to deal {@damage 1d10} Fire damage to that creature.
You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

feat.name: Infernal Dragoon
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Devilish Aura
entries: Your close association with diabolic powers unsettles others. You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to manifest an aura of fear in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from yourself. Each creature of your choice in that area makes a Charisma saving throw (DC 8 plus the modifier of the ability increased by this feat plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}). On a failed save, it has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, a creature is unaffected, and it is immune to this ability for 24 hours.

entries.name: Devil's Favor
entries: You can call on your infernal patron to aid you. When you make a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you can add a +2 bonus to the roll. Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Diabolic Empowerment
entries: You always have the {@spell Magic Weapon|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. When you cast it without a spell slot using this feature, the spell's duration becomes 8 hours for that casting. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

feat.name: Infernal Pact
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Infernal Resistance
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Fire damage and Poison damage.

entries.name: Infernal Sight
entries: You can see normally in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} and {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}—both magical and nonmagical—within 60 feet of yourself.

entries.name: Silver-Tongued
entries: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Deception|XPHB} skill.

spell.name: Astral Flood

components.m: a mixture of water and powdered silver

entries: You channel energy from the Astral Sea to unleash a torrent of magic from you in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. Each creature in the {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 4d10} Cold or Radiant damage (chosen when you cast this spell). Your choice of damage type determines an additional effect:

items.name: Cold Damage
entries: The target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the next {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} it makes before the end of your next turn.

items.name: Radiant Damage
entries: The target can see only within 15 feet of itself, and it has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition for everything beyond that distance until the end of your next turn.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d10} for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Buzzing Bee
entries: You call forth a noisy spectral bee. You target a creature that you can see within range. The bee hovers around the target, distracting it with loud buzzing. For the duration, the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks, Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks, and Constitution saving throws made to maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB}. In addition, the target cannot benefit from the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition.
The bee moves with the target, hovering within 3 feet of it as long as the target is within 120 feet of you. The bee disappears when the spell ends or if the target moves out of range.

spell.name: Insidious Rhythm
entries: One creature of your choice that you can see within range hears a haunting yet catchy tune that continuously repeats in its mind. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw, or it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Intelligence checks and on Constitution saving throws made to maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB}. At the end of each of its turns, the target repeats the save, ending the spell on itself on a success.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Leomund's Lamentable Belaborment
entries: You proclaim an insightful or inflammatory statement, such as a claim about the politics of a nearby town. Each creature in a 10-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on a point you choose within range must succeed on an Intelligence saving throw or have the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition until the spell ends. Any creature that doesn't share a language with you or that has an Intelligence score lower than 3 automatically succeeds on the saving throw.

While {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, a target must spend its turn discussing or arguing for or against your statement. For the duration, the target has a {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} of 0 and has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} and {@condition Deafened|XPHB} conditions with respect to everyone except you and other targets affected by this spell.
At the end of each of its turns, the target repeats the save, ending the spell on itself on a success.

spell.name: Sticks to Snakes

components.m: a small piece of bark and several snake scales

entries: You can choose up to four nonmagical sticks no longer than a {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} (or similar pieces of nonmagical wood, such as {@item Torch|XPHB|Torches}, {@item Spear|XPHB|Spears}, etc.) that you can see within range to shape-shift into snakes. Creatures that are holding a targeted object can make a Charisma saving throw, preventing the target from shape-shifting on a success. Each shape-shifted target uses the {@creature Venomous Snake|XMM} stat block.

Each of these snakes is {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly} to you and your allies. In combat, it shares your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count and takes its turn immediately after yours.

Until the spell ends, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to mentally command any creature you made with this spell if the creature is within 500 feet of you (if you control multiple creatures, you can command any of them at the same time, issuing the same command to each one). If you issue no commands, the creature takes the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action and moves only to avoid harm. When the creature drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it reverts to its object form, and any remaining damage carries over to that form.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: You can target two additional objects for each spell slot level above 4.

spell.name: Searing Orb

components.m: metallic flakes and a pinch of phosphorus

entries: You create and hurl a pulsing orb of energy at one creature within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 3d4} Radiant damage. Hit or miss, the orb then explodes in a flash of light. The target and each creature within 10 feet of it make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d4} for each spell slot level above 2.

spell.name: Tortoise Shell
entries: You touch a willing creature. Until the spell ends, the target's skin hardens into a tortoise shell, and the target gains a +3 bonus to AC. If the target moves, the bonus becomes +1 instead until the start of its next turn.

spell.name: Void Star
entries: You conjure forth a fragment of a dark star and launch it at one creature that you can see within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 6d12} Necrotic damage. At the end of the target's next turn, it takes {@damage 3d12} Necrotic damage, and you regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the amount of Necrotic damage dealt.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage (both initial and later) increases by {@dice 1d12} for each spell slot level above 7.

item.name: Amulet of Retributive Healing
entries: This amulet has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn. When you restore {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to one other creature, you can expend 1 charge to regain the same amount of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

item.name: Eternal Chalk
entries: A stick of Eternal Chalk never breaks or wears down with normal use. When using this chalk, you can choose the color of its marks and whether they emit a faint glow. Any marks you make with this chalk can't be erased for 7 days by anyone except you.

Whenever you regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} while wearing this armor, it takes on a reddish tint, and you gain a {=bonusAc} bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} until the end of your next turn.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The angel casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 18}).

spellcasting.name: Misty Step
headerEntries: The angel casts {@spell Misty Step|XPHB}, requiring no spell components and using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The angel has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The angel makes three Soul Scythe attacks.

action.name: Soul Scythe
entries: {@atkr m,r} {@hit 11}, reach 10 ft. or range 120 ft. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d8 + 6}) Slashing damage plus 13 ({@damage 3d8}) Necrotic or Radiant damage (angel's choice). If the target is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} by this attack, it is subjected to the following effect. {@actSave con} {@dc 18}. {@actSaveFail} The target dies, and its soul is trapped in the angel's scythe. The target can't be raised from the dead until the angel is destroyed, releasing all souls trapped in the scythe.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The angel casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 15}):

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The angel has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The angel makes two attacks, using Bite, Spear, or Radiant Ray in any combination.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 8}, reach 5 ft. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) Piercing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Radiant damage.

action.name: Spear
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 8}, reach 10 ft. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) Piercing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, the target is pushed up to 15 feet straight away from the angel.

action.name: Radiant Ray
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 7}, range 60 ft. {@h}14 ({@damage 3d6 + 4}) Radiant damage, and the target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 10 feet until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Overwhelming Presence {@recharge}
entries: {@actSave cha} {@dc 15}, each enemy in a 10-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on a point the angel can see within 120 feet. {@actSaveFail} 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Radiant damage, and the target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} and {@condition Stunned|XPHB} conditions until the end of the angel's next turn. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

bonus.name: Warding Bond (1/Day)
entries: The angel can choose one creature it can see within 30 feet to become its ward. The target becomes the angel's ward until it dies or until the angel uses this {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to choose a different ward. While the angel is within 10 feet of this ward, the ward gains a +4 bonus to saving throws.

reaction.name: Warding Parry
entries: {@actTrigger} The angel's ward is targeted by a melee or ranged attack while within 10 feet of the angel. {@actResponse} The ward adds 4 to its AC, and the angel makes a Spear attack against the triggering attacker if it is within reach.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The angel casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 22}):

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (4/Day)
entries: If the angel fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The angel has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The angel makes two Engulfing Slam attacks and uses Fling.

action.name: Engulfing Slam
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 17}, reach 20 ft. {@h}20 ({@damage 2d10 + 9}) Slashing damage plus 13 ({@damage 2d12}) Radiant damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is pulled into the angel's space and has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 19}). While {@condition Grappled|XPHB}, the target has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition. When the angel moves, the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} target moves with it, costing it no extra movement. The angel can have up to three creatures {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by this action at a time.

action.name: Fling
entries: The angel throws a Large or smaller creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by it to a space the angel can see within 30 feet of itself that isn't in the air. {@actSave dex} {@dc 25}, the creature thrown and each creature in the destination space. {@actSaveFail} 17 ({@damage 5d6}) Bludgeoning damage, and the target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

legendary.name: Fling
entries: The angel uses Fling. The angel can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

legendary.name: Spin and Slam
entries: The angel moves up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, and it makes one Engulfing Slam attack.

legendary.name: Whirlwind of Death
entries: {@actSave dex} {@dc 25}, each creature in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the angel. {@actSaveFail} 33 ({@damage 6d10}) Necrotic damage, and the target can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} until the end of the angel's next turn. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only. {@actSaveSuccessOrFail} The angel can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The angel casts one of the following spells, requiring no spell components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 15}):

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The angel has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The angel makes three Ring attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Unblinking Eye.

action.name: Ring
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 9}, reach 10 ft. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 15}) from one of five rings, and while {@condition Grappled|XPHB}, it has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition.

action.name: Cone of Retribution {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave wis} {@dc 15}, each enemy in a 90-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSaveFail} 18 ({@damage 4d8}) Radiant damage, and the target has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

action.name: Unblinking Eye
entries: {@actSave cha} {@dc 15}, one creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the angel. {@actSaveFail} 28 ({@damage 8d6}) Psychic damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.
Also called carperi, angels of death are sent to collect souls incredibly valuable to or desired by divine powers who don't want to risk the souls getting lost or intercepted.

Angels of death are often dispatched by their masters when those divine forces feel as though a mortal creature unfairly escaped a deserved death because of good fortune, unwarranted divine interference, or resurrection magic. These angels are often seen in the aftermath of accidents or other unfortunate events where someone survives against incredible odds.

Angels of death are patient, and they might watch a doomed creature for days or even weeks, waiting for a fatal blow to come. Doomed creatures may see out of the corners of their eyes the magically disguised angel lurking and hiding, but the angels disappear when the doomed tries to find them. If an angel fails to harvest the soul of its intended target, it pursues that target until the target is dead or the angel of death is defeated.

An angel of death is a six-foot-tall bipedal creature with a skull for a head and a desiccated, zombielike body. Its gangly limbs emerge from a tattered tunic and torn, dirty breeches. It holds an oversized scythe in two clawlike hands. The skeletal head wears a mask of an eerily smiling face of the same species it is pursuing. An angel of death often disguises itself as a humanoid in modest peasant clothing carrying a modest farming implement.

entries.name: Celestial Horrors
entries: Many commonfolk pray to the angels, lifting their tear-streaked faces to the sky in search of a powerful being who can fix their problems, remedy their grievances, or show them mercy. What the gods who answer those prayers fail to reveal is that the angels sent on their behalf are often more terrifying than the demons and devils everyone fears. These horrors are creatures of celestial provenance that are either terrifying in their natural form or warped versions of their benign selves through interactions with ever-demanding mortals.

If a devout member of a faith implores divine powers for help against an impending danger and is deemed worthy of protection, they might be assigned a guardian angel. This celestial being watches over its assigned mortal, destroying any threats.

If a guardian angel becomes corrupted through mistreatment, catastrophic failure at its task, or some other warping influence, it loses its sense of right and wrong, as well as its sense of proportionality. Such guardian angels become angels of obsession, also known as khulexi.

An angel of obsession becomes possessive of its ward, seeing everything and everyone near the ward as a threat, including friends and family. As soon as the ward harms or hinders the angel, the corrupted celestial turns on its ward, with anyone attempting to assist the ward also facing the angel's wrath.

An angel of obsession's true form is a three-headed bipedal creature with four wings. One of the heads is humanoid, while the other two heads are vicious and animalistic in nature; avians, bovines, canines, felines, and lupines are the most typical. When walking among mortals, an angel of obsession most often takes the visage of an armored warrior or attentive animal.

entries.name: Celestial Horrors
entries: Many commonfolk pray to the angels, lifting their tear-streaked faces to the sky in search of a powerful being who can fix their problems, remedy their grievances, or show them mercy. What the gods who answer those prayers fail to reveal is that the angels sent on their behalf are often more terrifying than the demons and devils everyone fears. These horrors are creatures of celestial provenance that are either terrifying in their natural form or warped versions of their benign selves through interactions with ever-demanding mortals.

Also known as myrmeriks, angels of slaughter serve gods of death (such as Myrkul in the Forgotten Realms setting or Nerull in the Greyhawk setting). Only three angels of slaughter exist at a time. Divine entities must bargain with angels of slaughter for their service.

These angels slaughter hundreds at a time in epic acts of carnage. Holy texts record these events as warnings against whole cities or nations earning the wrath of a god. In rare cases, certain individuals might earn the bitter enmity of a god, causing that deity to request the services of an angel of slaughter to destroy their quarry. Angels of slaughter often employ other creatures to assist in their slaughter, and they sometimes use weather events or plagues as cover for their atrocities.

Each angel of slaughter dwells in a fortress in the Shadowfell. In return for its services, an angel of slaughter is gifted some of the souls and corpses that it harvests during its catastrophic endeavors. The angel either consumes these souls to power its abilities, or the angel employs the souls to maintain its Shadowfell demesne.

In its true form, an angel of slaughter is a whirlwind of blood, bones, corpses, and blades. The angel often takes the illusionary form of a giant, a massive beast, or another spectral entity to survey the area the angel is about to decimate. Many mortals recognize sightings of an angel of slaughter as portents for coming disasters.

entries.name: Celestial Horrors
entries: Many commonfolk pray to the angels, lifting their tear-streaked faces to the sky in search of a powerful being who can fix their problems, remedy their grievances, or show them mercy. What the gods who answer those prayers fail to reveal is that the angels sent on their behalf are often more terrifying than the demons and devils everyone fears. These horrors are creatures of celestial provenance that are either terrifying in their natural form or warped versions of their benign selves through interactions with ever-demanding mortals.

entries.name: Angel of Slaughter Lairs
entries: Each angel of slaughter has a unique lair in the Shadowfell. In addition to the gloom and despair of the Shadowfell, these lairs are littered with the corpses and haunted by the spirits of those destroyed by the angel.

Angels of vengeance, also known as umulti, begin their existences as planetars, sent by greater divine powers to seek justice and retribution for those wronged. If a planetar performs evil, immoral, or unsavory acts while delivering justice, it can become corrupted. Its evil acts cause it to waste away physically and spiritually, transforming into an angel of vengeance.

Angels of vengeance target those who commit transgressions against divine entities, often regardless of the transgression's severity. These angels aim to kill, not to parley, and an angel of vengeance speaks only to let a victim know that they are about to die.

An angel of vengeance's true form is large fiery eye within the center of five concentric rings. The rings are often made of a material that befits the divine power that it originally worked for; for example, angels created by fire deities evoke rings of fire, while those created by wealth deities often bear rings of precious metals. Angels of vengeance often disguise themselves as silent, purposeful humanoids clad in armor and wielding swords.
